model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Illinois enters this matchup with the nation's most efficient offense, a unit that has consistently overwhelmed opponents with its combination of elite shot-making and transition play. While North Carolina remains a formidable opponent with a top-35 analytical profile, the season-ending injury to star freshman Caleb Wilson significantly limits their frontcourt versatility and defensive ceiling. Although 7-footer Henri Veesaar provides a reliable scoring presence for the Tar Heels, they likely lack the defensive consistency required to stifle an Illinois squad that ranks in the top 25 nationally on both ends of the floor. Expect the Illini's superior offensive firepower and depth to be the difference in a high-scoring affair.

reasoningStep.title: Illinois' Elite Offense vs. Shorthanded North Carolina
reasoningStep.summary: Illinois' #1 ranked offensive efficiency and balanced profile give them a significant advantage over a North Carolina team missing its star freshman, Caleb Wilson.
evidence: Illinois ranks 6th/7th in advanced metrics with the #1/2 adjusted offensive efficiency.
North Carolina ranks 27th/29th and is navigating the loss of Caleb Wilson.
Henri Veesaar (16.7 ppg) is UNC's primary option in Wilson's absence.
Illinois' top-25 defense and superior overall talent make them the clear favorites.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
